text: 七転び八起き — fall down seven times, get up eight.
An old pond — a frog leaps in, the sound of water.
O snail, climb Mount Fuji, but slowly, slowly!
Sitting quietly, doing nothing, spring comes, and the grass grows by itself.
The temple bell stops — but the sound keeps coming out of the flowers.
Barn's burnt down — now I can see the moon.
In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities, but in the expert's there are few.
If you cannot find the truth right where you are, where else do you expect to find it?
The bamboo that bends is stronger than the oak that resists.
This dewdrop world is a dewdrop world — and yet, and yet.
The thief left it behind: the moon at my window.
Confine yourself to the present.
No great thing is created suddenly.
It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.
When you have completed 95 percent of your journey, you are only halfway there.
Before enlightenment: chop wood, carry water. After enlightenment: chop wood, carry water.
Even monkeys fall from trees.
The day you decide to do it is your lucky day.
A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
Each of you is perfect the way you are — and you can use a little improvement.
When walking, walk. When eating, eat.
Winter solitude — in a world of one colour, the sound of wind.
In this world we walk on the roof of hell, gazing at flowers.
Nothing lasts, nothing is finished, nothing is perfect.
The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
One kind word can warm three winter months.
Morning glory! The well bucket entangled, I ask for water.
Let go or be dragged.
Every day is a journey, and the journey itself is home.
Vision without action is a daydream. Action without vision is a nightmare.
